name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
resistance_profile: Isolates shoulder extension (lats) with a stretch overhead.
cues: Pull the bar to the thighs via the lats
execution_steps: Stand facing a high pulley with a straight bar, arms extended overhead and elbows fixed at a slight bend.
Hinge slightly forward and brace your core.
Keeping the elbows locked, drive the bar down in an arc to your thighs using the lats.
Squeeze the lats at the bottom, then return under control to the overhead stretch.
good_when: You want to isolate the lats and groove the elbow-down pull before pulldowns.
You want a constant-tension lat finisher that avoids biceps involvement.
bad_when: You want a primary back-mass builder, where a pulldown or row loads more muscle.
You want to lift heavy for strength, since isolation limits useful load.
